mnemonics.title: B-TIC Construction
mnemonics.content: Build JButtons using: Basic (empty), Text, Icon, Combination - progression of constructors from simple to complex

mnemonics.title: LIST for Events
mnemonics.content: Listener Interface > Implement actionPerformed > Set via addActionListener > Trigger on click. Sequence for event handling

mnemonics.title: EDRP States
mnemonics.content: Remember button states: Enabled, Disabled, Rollover, Pressed. Like elevator button states

mnemonics.title: MVC Layers
mnemonics.content: Model (data) <-> View (button) <-> Controller (logic). Imagine three-layer architecture cake

acronyms.expansion: Border, Underline, Text, Tooltip, Orientation, Name
Assign Listener, Create Event, Trigger Method, Implement Logic, Override Methods, Notify Components

visualTips.visualization: Imagine JButton as bottom of inheritance tree: JButton → AbstractButton → JComponent → Container → Component → Object
Visualize click event as electrical current flowing from button → listener → actionPerformed method
Picture Model as brain, View as face, Controller as nervous system connecting them
Imagine four traffic lights: Green (enabled), Red (disabled), Yellow (rollover), Flashing (pressed)

analogies.analogy: Like a pizza delivery hotline - when button (phone) is pressed, listener (operator) triggers action (dispatch)
Car dashboard: Model (speed data), View (speedometer display), Controller (mechanisms updating display)
Movie ticket machine: Click (select movie) → Event (payment processed) → Action (print ticket)
